subjects.name: Eventbrite

typography_hierarchy.note: Modern sans hierarchy is clean and consistent across discovery, listing and checkout flows.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Add a display weight for hero category headers and tighten leading on long event descriptions.

layout_information_architecture.note: Proven 3-tier IA: discovery feed, event detail, checkout — well-trodden paths and predictable patterns.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Add explicit P2P-organiser onboarding lane separate from the consumer event-discovery surface.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Orange-on-white feels generic SaaS; the brand is recognised but not loved.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Develop an editorial photography system that elevates community events above stock-image aesthetic.

content_credibility_proof.note: Massive event volume + organiser testimonials + organic SEO authority build trust by default.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Surface escrow / refund-protection badges prominently — current platform has neither.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Fast LCP, clean responsive layouts, native-feeling check-in app and ticket scanning.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Ship offline-tolerant ticket display and cleaner Apple Wallet integration.

subjects.verdict: Category default with mature tooling; brand and trust artefacts (escrow) are the weak spots that The Commons can wedge against.

subjects.name: Peatix

typography_hierarchy.note: Functional sans, decent hierarchy but feels JP-corporate carry-over on web.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Modernise the type scale and introduce a friendlier display face for community events.

layout_information_architecture.note: Discovery feed prioritises chronology over interest; categorisation feels under-developed.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Persona-led entry points (community / hobby / professional) and saved interest filters.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Friendly green-and-white but indistinct from generic event-tech competitors.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Lean into community-first warmth — bespoke illustration system, real-organiser photography.

content_credibility_proof.note: Strong SG community usage but visible proof artefacts on-site are limited.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Show organiser case studies, repeat-attendee badges, group continuity counts above the fold.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Native iOS/Android apps are solid; web mobile experience is dated by comparison.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Web parity with the native apps and a clean PWA install path.

subjects.verdict: Good SG community footprint but the digital experience feels 2018; brand and discovery UX both lag the leaders.

subjects.name: Klook

typography_hierarchy.note: Confident type system, strong product-card hierarchy, multi-language coverage.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Refine long-form review-content typography for tablet-and-up reading.

layout_information_architecture.note: Best-in-class experience-marketplace IA — destination, category, vetted operator structure is rock solid.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Expose more peer-reviewed UX cues in category landing pages.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Vivid orange + lifestyle photography is unmistakable across SE Asia.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Continue editorial leadership — branded long-form travel storytelling.

content_credibility_proof.note: Massive review volume, named operators, partnership badges, media mentions all surfaced.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Show operator-side onboarding standards publicly to build attendee confidence further.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile-first by design; sub-2s LCP on most listings; PayNow checkout is seamless.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Push native app further with offline voucher access in poor signal.

subjects.verdict: Category-leading mobile commerce execution. The Commons cannot beat Klook on curated experience commerce — must differentiate on P2P + escrow + open peer hosting.

subjects.name: Luma

typography_hierarchy.note: Considered serif-meets-sans pairing; calendar invites and event pages feel editorial, not transactional.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Maintain identity discipline as feature surface expands.

layout_information_architecture.note: Calendar-native model is a category innovation — RSVP flows feel like email-native invites.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Better recurring-event tooling for community organisers.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Confident, soft-modern brand voice that distances Luma from Eventbrite-era SaaS aesthetic.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Stronger localisation in non-US markets without diluting the core voice.

content_credibility_proof.note: Tech-community organic adoption is strong proof; explicit trust artefacts (refunds, escrow) are missing.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Surface organiser reliability stats and refund-policy clarity, especially for paid events.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Crisp mobile RSVP flow, calendar-app integration, fast everywhere.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Native apps to lock in the calendar-first habit.

subjects.verdict: The strongest design taste in the category. The Commons must learn from Luma's editorial restraint while pushing harder on community warmth and SG localisation.

subjects.name: Venuerific

typography_hierarchy.note: Functional venue-listing typography; hero is competent but unremarkable.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Editorialise venue stories and improve scannability on long category pages.

layout_information_architecture.note: Solid filter / category structure for venue discovery; weak organiser-tooling layer because none exists.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Expose end-to-end booking workflow rather than parking enquiries offline.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Orange-and-white SaaS palette; lifestyle photography is functional but not aspirational.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Bespoke commissioned venue photography with consistent moods per category.

content_credibility_proof.note: Long history in SG, named venues, real reviews build trust.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Add escrow on booking deposits and surface refund / cancellation outcomes transparently.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile usable with reasonable performance; some long pages feel desktop-first.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Mobile-first reflow of long venue category pages and faster image delivery.

subjects.verdict: Adjacent player solving venue discovery — strong SG SEO, weak transactional layer. The Commons can integrate venues, not compete on venues.

subjects.name: The Commons (self)

typography_hierarchy.note: Inter sans + bold weights deliver a confident, modern hierarchy; daiquiri-beach palette stays legible at scale.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Pair Inter with a softer display face for emotional event-page hero sections; tighten long-form FAQ leading.

layout_information_architecture.note: 4-pillar IA (Discover / Create / Marketplace / Dashboard) is intuitive; admin Atlas adds depth without crowding the consumer surface.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Add an explicit immediate-need lane (last-minute event find) and persistent vendor-marketplace entry from event creation flow.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Sunset-coral + ocean-turquoise + tropical-palm palette with sand neutrals is unmistakably daiquiri-beach — distinct from every event-platform peer.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Commission bespoke event photography in the daiquiri-beach palette and develop a signature motion language for transitions.

content_credibility_proof.note: Escrow narrative + Elitez Group backing + daiquiri-beach voice are stated but not yet anchored with audited artefacts.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Ship an audited escrow flow doc, MAS PSA compliance posture, vendor onboarding standards page, and live event-success-rate counter.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile reflows cleanly with consistent type and CTAs; some hero images and event cards still benefit from compression.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Compress hero assets, ship PWA install path, target <2.5s LCP across discovery + event-detail + checkout.

subjects.verdict: Visual brand already matches the design leaders; credibility artefacts (escrow audit, vendor standards) and mobile performance polish are the missing tenths.

synthesis.passage_position: The Commons matches Eventbrite at 7.4 for category lead on visual brand and typography, ahead of Peatix (6.0) and Venuerific (6.6) on every dimension. Behind only Klook (8.8) and Luma (8.4) — both on credibility / proof depth and mobile-performance polish.
easy_wins_for_passage: Surface the escrow narrative above the fold on every event-detail page — currently lives only on the marketplace pillar.
Add a 'Why The Commons' trust strip to the homepage hero with the 4 differentiators (escrow, marketplace, daiquiri-beach SG-first, Elitez-backed).
Compress hero imagery and ship a PWA install path to get mobile LCP under 2.5s across discovery + checkout.
Publish a one-page vendor-onboarding standards doc + an event-success-rate counter (eg. '98% of events delivered as promised — across 1,200 events to date').
ten_x_aspirations: Calendar-native invite flow that beats Luma in SG by integrating PayNow + RSVP + escrow in the email itself.
Live escrow-reserve counter — show how much member money is currently held safely, refresh in real time, audited monthly.
Bespoke daiquiri-beach photography system commissioned across SG event categories (community, party, hobby, sport, supper club) — make every event card look like the cover of a magazine.
Vendor reputation graph — verified completion-rate, response-time SLA, and dispute history per vendor, surfaced before booking.
